characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Our main hero,flawed ashe is. Son of the legendary Slickis, he has a lot to live up to... but hisUgly Cuteness, nervous disposition and self-confidence issues combined with his tendency to slack off keep working against him. Still, one shouldn't underestimate him based on his looks or personal problems; hisscaringskillsare nothing to laugh at... Acquired Situational Narcissism: Give him a situation where hetemporarilygets popularity, riches or notoriety... and about five seconds. Hello again,cocky-assIckis! Aesop Amnesia:Played With. Ickis goes through multiple stories where he getsAcquired Situational Narcissismand then things immediately end up going wrong for him. However, each story has Ickis getting cocky for a different reason depending on the story. For example; he completes a successful scare and gets praised by the Gromble and Dizzle, he's farther along on his homework than Oblina for a change, he finds a bunch of toenails and is temporarily wealthy, or he's new in school and thinks having a famous father automatically means he will be respected and revered simply for existing. The Aesop is never presented as a blanket "don't be cocky" but instead as "don't be cocky if *insert situation* happens", so it's likely Ickis never makes the connection that cockiness all by itself is annoying or wrong. I Am Not Weasel: Humans often mistake him for a bunny, much to his annoyance. Badass Adorable: Ickis is a nice guy and he looks a lot like a cartoony rabbit. Butdon'ttick him off, because he can be just as terrifying as his old man if he gets seriously motivated. In particular, his natural knack for theSizeshiftertechnique makes him a physical powerhouse.

characters.name: Ickis
characters.description: Our main hero,flawed ashe is. Son of the legendary Slickis, he has a lot to live up to... but hisUgly Cuteness, nervous disposition and self-confidence issues combined with his tendency to slack off keep working against him. Still, one shouldn't underestimate him based on his looks or personal problems; hisscaringskillsare nothing to laugh at... Acquired Situational Narcissism: Give him a situation where hetemporarilygets popularity, riches or notoriety... and about five seconds. Hello again,cocky-assIckis! Aesop Amnesia:Played With. Ickis goes through multiple stories where he getsAcquired Situational Narcissismand then things immediately end up going wrong for him. However, each story has Ickis getting cocky for a different reason depending on the story. For example; he completes a successful scare and gets praised by the Gromble and Dizzle, he's farther along on his homework than Oblina for a change, he finds a bunch of toenails and is temporarily wealthy, or he's new in school and thinks having a famous father automatically means he will be respected and revered simply for existing. The Aesop is never presented as a blanket "don't be cocky" but instead as "don't be cocky if *insert situation* happens", so it's likely Ickis never makes the connection that cockiness all by itself is annoying or wrong. I Am Not Weasel: Humans often mistake him for a bunny, much to his annoyance. Badass Adorable: Ickis is a nice guy and he looks a lot like a cartoony rabbit. Butdon'ttick him off, because he can be just as terrifying as his old man if he gets seriously motivated. In particular, his natural knack for theSizeshiftertechnique makes him a physical powerhouse.

characters.name: Krumm
characters.description: Ickis's best friend and fellow underachiever, a squat, hairy, peach-colored monster withfree-rolling eyesthat he normally carried around in his hands. Cheerful and carefree, he never stressed about scaring as much as his roommates, just enjoying whatever misadventures they got into. Affably Evil: He's a monster, but he's generally pretty nice. Big, Thin, Short Trio: The Big to Oblina's Thin and Ickis' Short. Bizarre Alien Biology: His eyes aren't even attached to his body, but somehow they still provide him vision nontheless. Brilliant, but Lazy: Krumm is actually more intelligent than he lets on (much to the surprise of The Gromble), to the point where he's actually scored second behind Oblina in scaring at least once. He only acts dumb because there's less pressure on himself that way.

characters.name: Oblina
characters.description: Though her family had enough toenails that she could lounge in the lap of luxury for the rest of her life, Oblina chose to attend the Monster Academy. Since she couldpull her insides out, she quickly became top scarer in their class, despite having two of theworststudents as her roommates. Frequently tries to get them to take their studies more seriously, and isn't above gloating whenever ignoring her advice gets them in trouble... but isn't above the antics herself, andstill considers them her best friends. Action Girl: Or the scarier equivalent. Balloon Belly: Because of how slender she is, when she eats something, it tends to visibly bulge through her skin as she swallows. A focal point in"Internal Affairs", in which she becomes infested by a parasite-monster that forces her to consume random junk to decorate his new home in her stomach, to the point it's feared she'll eventually rupture something. Becoming the Mask: Happened once; while disguised as a human to gather information on them, she had a bad reaction to cotton candy and ended up believing she was really human. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Ickis, the two snipe at each other all the time but it's clear they're attracted to each other.

characters.name: The Gromble
characters.description: Headmaster and head teacher, The Gromble certainly isn't afraid to berate, belittle and punish his students whenever they falter in their scaring or studies. Particularly likes to single out Ickis and his friends — though admittedly, theydobring a lot of it down on themselves. For all his harshness, he really does care about his students and wants to see them succeed — even if he has to beat every scrap of hard work out of them! Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: He wears a pair of black gloves, a black belt, and of course, four red pumps. Agony of the Feet: In the first iteration of hisMultiple-Choice Past, the Gromble acquired his trademark high heels as a kid and has kept them ever since... even though he's outgrown the size he insists on wearing. His former teacher, the Shroink, explicitly tells Ickis, Krumm, and Oblina that the Gromble is so cranky all the time because his toes are pinched. Badass Teacher: When he's not being aSadist Teacher, he does prove to be helpful toward his students when they need him. Camp Straight: He was melodramatic and wore red pumps, but in one episode had a crush on the female librarian.

characters.name: The Snorch
characters.description: The Unintelligiblemonster who handles all theCool And Unusual Punishmentsrule-breaking students have to suffer. Very intelligent, with a love for the very same things he uses as punishments, like opera and square dancing. Unfortunately, the communication barrier makes it difficult to share his interests and thoughts with others... Berserk Button: Don't mess with his golden nosehair... he's very protective of it. The main trio figure this out the hard way. Big Guy, Little Guy: The big to Zimbo's little. Cool and Unusual Punishment: His specialty. He’ll make you clean shoes and listen to bad music amongst many others. "Flowers for Algernon" Syndrome: In "I Heard the Snorch Call My Name" he's fitted with an electronic voice box that allows him to speak normally, revealing himself to be a smart and sensitive creature. Unfortunately, it doesn't last and he's quite downcast when he returns to normal.

characters.name: Zimbo
characters.description: As the only monster capable of understanding The Snorch — and a craven little coward with a tiny body and no real physical strength of his own — Zimbo likes to stick close to The Snorch and leech off his fearsome reputation. Also uses his size to his advantage by spying on the students and reporting back to The Gromble. Big Guy, Little Guy: The little to The Snorch's big. Green and Mean: A green-haired jerk. The Informant: To the Snorch and Gromble. If anything comes up, he’ll report it. Jerkass: In spades. Ickis freaked out about a cute movie? He freaks him out more to the point ofpotentially getting him institutionalized.

characters.name: Hairyette
characters.description: A background student in the Monster Academy and a friend of Oblina. Bat People: As only shown in "Slumber Scare", Hairyette's scaring method is separating her body segments and turning her arms into bat wings. Graceful Ladies Like Purple: She wears a skirt which is usually colored purple, but it is also sometimes blue or pink. Pom-Pom Girl: She is on the school's cheerleading team.

characters.name: Horrifica
characters.description: A monster who has several appendages on her head, three eyes, and a teal dress. Out of Focus: Her first starring role was in "Krumm's Pimple", but she was only shown in the first half. Pom-Pom Girl: She is on the school's cheerleading team. Third Eye: Horrificia has three eyes.

characters.name: Zabliner
characters.description: A gray-colored monster who has a crescent-shaped head. The Voiceless: Zabliner hasneverspoken in the show. The only sound they've made is a rasberry in "Ship of Fools''. Viewer Gender Confusion: Zabliner's gender is unknown since they've never talked in the show and are never referred to by name.

characters.name: Slickis
characters.description: Ickis's father and the most famous graduate of Monster Academy. Living up to his legacy is quite the responsibility... The Ace: Played straight, as he's a naturally gifted scarer and athlete and is aNice Guywho's well-liked by almost everyone. On the other hand, he has someHidden Depthssuggesting he's not that different from his son. Acrofatic: Flashbacks show him as a chubby bonsty, and even nowadays he's quite a bit broader and heavier than Ickis. Yet Slickis still has an easy time scaring humans, can stand up to a tank, and is great at monster sports (evidenced when he bowls a perfect game of Gorblats.) He might be aBig Eateras he cheerfully suggests buying a large amount of Slime Pies, and his first choice when listing father/son activities was to go forage for food. Boisterous Bruiser: Extremely friendly, cheerful, and happy, making yet another stark contrast with his emotional son. Generation Xerox: Basically an older Ickis with a scar on one of the ears.

characters.name: Horvak
characters.description: Krumm's father, an equally cheerful monster who makes his living as a muck farmer. Has only one eye, and wears a black glove over his empty hand. Curse: Like his son, suffered the effects of the Curse of the Krumm.Unlikehis son, was never able to break it, andlost his stench for good. Eyepatch of Power: Wore a black glove over the hand that was supposed to hold his lost eye. Eye Scream/Historical In-Joke: He attempted to scare people in pre-Revolutionary America, but dropped one of his eyes as a soldier dropped a box of musket balls. The soldier grabbed Horvak's eye by mistake, loaded it, and... well, it was the Shot Heard Round the World. Fantastic Racism: He doesn't want Krumm to attend class with a combustible monster, and tries to come up with an excuse for Krumm to leave.

characters.name: Sublima
characters.description: Oblina's rich and overbearing mother. She disapproves of her daughter becoming a common scarer, and would much rather see her return to the lap of luxury. My Beloved Smother: She's dismissive of Oblina's desire to be a scarer and insists she knows what's best for her. Not to mention how often she reminds Oblina to refer to her as "Mumsy dearest." However, she's willing to concede when both Oblina and Skeech call her out on how overbearing she is. My God, What Have I Done?: Sublima legitimately shows remorse when her husband and daughter both read her the riot act for her domineering attitude and vows to be more respectful towards Oblina's goals. No Celebrities Were Harmed: Charlie Adler is clearly doing a combination ofBette DavisandJoan Crawfordin regards to Sublima's accent, inflections, and her desire to be called "Mumsy dearest" by Oblina.Oblina: Yes, Mumsy.Sublima: Yes, Mumsywhat?Oblina: Yes, Mumsydearest!Sublima: Better. Rich Bitch: Looks down upon other monsters who aren't as wealthy, and thinks that her daughter's dreams of becoming a great scarer are beneath her.

characters.name: Skeech
characters.description: Oblina's meek and submissive father, who only wants to see his wife and daughter happy. Henpecked Husband: Sublima frequently speaks over him, but he gains the courage to stand up to her after Oblina does so. Open-Minded Parent: He is in favor of his daughter's scaring career and stands up to his wife to allow Oblina to return to the Academy. So Proud of You: He states this of Oblina after she calls Sublima out for being too controlling.

characters.name: Simon D. Hunter
characters.description: As his title implies, Simon is a self-declared monster hunter, determined to break the masquerade and prove once and for all that he isn't insane. Develops a very personal grudge against Ickis after the little monster thwarts his plans and keeps humiliating him. His surname and middle initial were given in his expired driver's license in the episode "Battle of the Century." Beware the Silly Ones: He's hammy as can be and can be quite ditzy, but he's come up with his fair share of plans that put the monsters on defense. He's also independently wealthy, so he has the resources to construct various devices and implement his plans. Card-Carrying Villain: In "Simon Strikes Back," he describes himself as "bad" and "worse" and eventuallychristens himselfSimon the Terrible. Even Evil Has Standards: Despite constantly trying to capture the monsters, Simon means no particular harm to them. In the episode "Showdown" he even rescues Ickis from some more ruthless monster hunters who want to kill Ickis instead of capturing him alive. Final Boss: He serves as this inthe video game adaptationfor theSNESandSega Genesis. He launches nets at the monsters, grabs him with his claw if they get too close to him, and can only be damaged when they scare him. Hitting him with a projectile causes him to drop a Monster Manual, which allows the monsters to perform their scares.

characters.name: Bradley
characters.description: A somewhat nerdy young boy who became curious about monsters after first encountering them. Briefly became Simon's apprentice; however, when Ickis told himwhat would happen ifmonsterkind was revealed to humanity, he promptlyhelped them escapeand became aSecret-Keeper. Chekhov's Gunman: He doesn’t seem like much aside from almost getting Ickis in trouble at first, but he ends up slightly more important later on in the season. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Reappears only once after hisHeel–Face Turn. Heel–Face Turn: Goes from hunting Ickis to being one of his allies. Secret-Keeper: One of the few people that knows about monsters.
